LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN FITZPATRICK VANNUCCI IN PRO PER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. DEFENDANT VANNUCCI'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FILED ITS AMENDED COMPLAINT ON JANUARY 25, 2019, GIVING VANNUCCI UNTIL FEBRUARY 25 TO FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING. (CCP 471.5.) PLAINTIFF MOVED FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT FOUR DAYS LATER, MARCH 1, THOUGH IT KNEW VANNUCCI WAS ACTIVELY LITIGATING THE CASE. EVEN IF CRC 3.1320 APPLIED HERE, THE DIFFERENCE WOULD BE A MATTER OF DAYS. QUICK-PITCHING A DEFAULT IS A SHARP PRACTICE THAT THE COURTS ABJURE. (SEE WEITZ V. YANKOSKY (1966) 63 CAL.2D 849, 855; FASUYI V. PERMATEX, INC. (2008) 167 CAL.APP.4TH 681, 701.) COUNSEL ARE URGED TO MEET AND CONFER BEFORE FILING ANY FURTHER MOTIONS AND TO TREAT ONE ANOTHER AS IS BECOMING OF OFFICERS OF THE COURT. VANNUCCI'S RESPONSIVE PLEADING SHALL BE FILED BY APRIL 17, 2019. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN FITZPATRICK VANNUCCI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Defendant John Fitzpatrick Vannucci's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Fast Track Investment Company LLC's first amended complaint is denied as to the prayer for compensatory damages and is granted without leave to amend as to the prayer for attorney fees. As the court previously ruled in denying Mr. Vanucci's motion to strike the prayer for relief in the original complaint, the amount of compensatory damages is adequately alleged (FAC 21) and Fast Trak need not plead evidentiary facts to support those allegations.
The prayer for attorney fees against Mr. Vanucci is not supported by the facts alleged on the face of the complaint which include the matters shown in exhibits attached to the complaint. Mr. Vanucci is not a signatory to the Agreement between Fast Trak and Ms. Reed, and the Acknowledgment signed by Mr. Vanucci does not have an attorney fee provision and does not incorporate by reference the attorney fee provision from the Agreement. (See Barnett v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2001) 90 Cal.App.4th 500, 505 ["to the extent the factual allegations conflict with the content of the exhibits to the complaint, we rely on and accept as true the contents of the exhibits and treat as surplusage the pleader's allegations as to the legal effect of the exhibits."].

The reciprocity language of Civ. Code 1717 (a) does not support Fast Trak's contention that it can enforce a contractual attorney fee provision against a non-party to the contract. Section 1717 safeguards mutuality of remedy in two circumstances, neither of which applies here. (See California-American Water Co. v. Marina Coast Water Dist. (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 571, 577.) Berge v. International Harvester Co. (1983) 142 Cal.App.3d 152 is distinguishable, because there the plaintiff and cross-defendant was a party to a retail sales contract containing an attorney's fees clause and her opponent was the assignee of the contract.

Because Fast Trak has not shown that further amendment is capable of curing the deficiency in its pleading, the motion to strike is granted without leave to amend.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Apr-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court received a late contest email on 4/11 at 8:09 pm, however no one appeared at the 9:30 am hearing today. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JOHN FITZPATRICK VANNUCCI IN PRO PER's Motion To Set Aside Default.

Defendant Vannucci's motion to set aside default is GRANTED. Plaintiff filed its amended complaint on January 25, 2019, giving Vannucci until February 25 to file a responsive pleading. (CCP 471.5.) Plaintiff moved for entry of default four days later, March 1, though it knew Vannucci was actively litigating the case. Even if CRC 3.1320 applied here, the difference would be a matter of days. Quick-pitching a default is a sharp practice that the courts abjure. (See Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 855; Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 701.) Counsel are urged to meet and confer before filing any further motions and to treat one another as is becoming of officers of the court. Vannucci's responsive pleading shall be filed by April 17, 2019.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
